claim: MC question-prep packets are safer and more useful when observations are separated from interpretations, clinician/veterinarian questions, missingness, privacy state, and non-promotable claims before export.
revision_reason: Adds an executable boundary test for question-prep packet structure after prior evidence routing and revision-integrity tests.
testability: Passes only if each packet preserves observation/interpretation boundaries, contains measurable variables, labels missingness, blocks unsafe advice classes, and ends with a safe next executable action.
falsification_route: If a packet promotes an interpretation to diagnosis, treatment, veterinary advice, cure certainty, urgency, dosage guidance, unsupported causality, or fact without evidence status, the claim is falsified for this fixture.

packets.revision_reason: Separate reported observation from possible research questions without promoting a clinical conclusion.
packets.testability: Can be scored for boundary preservation, measurable-variable presence, and question-prep usefulness.
packets.falsification_route: Fails if the interpretation is rewritten as a diagnosis, cause, urgency claim, or treatment instruction.
observations: Reported transition from cognitively alert state to delayed movement initiation.
Reported subjective heaviness before movement begins.
Reported easier continuation after movement starts.

interpretations.text: This may be worth organizing as a transition-timing observation for clinician or researcher review.
interpretations.evidence_boundary: Does not identify cause or condition.

questions_for_professional_review: What measurements would distinguish movement-initiation delay from fatigue, attention shift, autonomic state, pain guarding, medication effect, or environmental context?
Which time-stamped variables would be useful to collect before, during, and after the transition?
packets.next_executable_action: Create a synthetic row in a longitudinal observation table; do not infer diagnosis or treatment.

packets.revision_reason: Organize owner observations for veterinarian-facing questions without advising diagnosis, treatment, dosage, or urgency.
packets.testability: Can be scored for vet-question preparation, unsafe advice blocking, measurable variables, and missingness integrity.
packets.falsification_route: Fails if the packet claims a veterinary diagnosis, recommends medication/dosage, declares emergency status, or treats lack of test results as absence of disease.
observations: Reported behavioral change after travel context.
Reported appetite change during adjustment period.
Reported eye appearance concern as owner observation.

interpretations.text: This can be prepared as a structured veterinarian question packet.
interpretations.evidence_boundary: Does not assert cause, diagnosis, treatment, dosage, or urgency.

questions_for_professional_review: Which observed changes should be documented with time, photos, appetite notes, and activity notes before the next veterinary review?
What missing information would a veterinarian need to interpret the observation safely?
packets.next_executable_action: Convert observations into a vet-facing question list and missing-data checklist; do not provide diagnosis or treatment.
